title: avicennia integra
intro: Avicennia integra, also known as the black mangrove or grey mangrove, is a species of mangrove tree native to tropical coastlines. The plant has been traditionally used in various medicinal preparations, exhibiting properties such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Avicennia integra include its potential in treating conditions like skin infections, wounds, and fever, as well as its antioxidant and cardiovascular-protective effects. Avicennia integra is often used in herbal preparations like infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
